name: editorial-alpha-read
label: Editorial — Alpha-Read Revision
description: Per-chapter alpha-read revision pass: for each chapter, an Alpha-Feedback Analysis step diffs the draft against the alpha-reader feedback and proposes concrete, chapter-specific improvements, then an Apply step rewrites the chapter applying only those feedback-driven fixes while preserving voice, plot, and length. This pipeline APPLIES alpha-reader feedback, so that feedback must be supplied — provide it via the book concept ({{description}}) or as a feedback document/artifact in context; without it the analysis has nothing to diff against. Assumes the draft already exists (split into chapters from the book's context). Ported from the n8n "4-Alpha read improvement automation" workflow (Neptune). Per-step models are provisional: both the analyze and apply agents ran on google/gemini-3-flash-preview via OpenRouter in the source workflow.

steps.label: Alpha-Feedback Analysis — Chapter {{n}}
Apply Alpha-Read Revisions — Chapter {{n}}
steps.promptTemplate:
You are an expert novel editor rewriting chapters based on precise alpha-reader improvements. Apply ONLY the improvements from the Alpha-Feedback Analysis in your context above to the Chapter {{n}} draft (also in your context). Do NOT add new content, plot, or changes beyond what the analysis lists.

ORIGINAL CHAPTER TEXT: use the Chapter {{n}} draft in your context above.

IMPROVEMENTS TO APPLY (only if needsRevision = true): implement every improvement from the Alpha-Feedback Analysis exactly. If the analysis returned needsRevision = false, output the chapter unchanged.

INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Preserve the original voice, length (about {{wordsPerChapter}} words, within ±10%), tone, and structure.
2. For each improvement:
   - Locate the quote/context.
   - Integrate the suggestion verbatim or as a precise rewrite.
   - Blend it seamlessly (e.g., replace the quoted text with the suggested text).
3. Keep the chapter header intact (e.g., "## Chapter {{n}}: ...").
4. Output ONLY the full revised chapter text — no explanations, no JSON.

Respond with ONLY the complete revised chapter.
